message:
fix(http): RFC 9110 identity encoding negotiation and dedup size check

Two fixes in content encoding negotiation:

1. Identity encoding: per RFC 9110, identity is acceptable by default
   unless explicitly refused via identity;q=0 or a zero-quality wildcard
   (*;q=0) without a more specific identity entry. Previously, identity
   was unconditionally acceptable when not mentioned, even when *;q=0
   should have excluded it.

2. Decompressor size limit: extracted the decompressed-size overflow
   check into update_decompressed_total() helper, eliminating duplicated
   code across IdentityDecompressor and GzipDecompressor (and any future
   decompressors).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
